name: Hippie Jump
history: The skater jumps over an obstacle (bar, fence, rail) while the board rolls under it, then lands back on the board on the other side. A unique trick requiring timing.

name: Caveman
history: The skater holds the board and jumps while running, lands on it mid-flight. A trick from the early days of street skating often used to approach obstacles.

name: Running Start
history: Gaining speed by running alongside the board before jumping on and ollie-ing a gap. Used to approach very large gaps or rails with maximum speed.
youtube_query: running start ollie skateboard gap trick

name: Shove-it to Grind
history: A pop shove-it that enters a grind. The 180-degree board rotation is used to set up the grind approach.
youtube_query: shove it to grind skateboard tutorial

name: Shove-it to Nosegrind
history: A pop shove-it into a nosegrind. The board rotates and the nose locks onto the obstacle.

name: Nollie Flip to 5-0
history: A nollie kickflip that lands in a 5-0 grind. A technical combination of flip entry and grind balance.

name: Tre Flip to Nosegrind
history: A 360 flip into a nosegrind. One of the most technical ledge combination tricks.
youtube_query: tre flip to nosegrind skateboard tutorial

name: Laserflip to Noseslide
history: A laser flip that lands in a noseslide. An exceptionally rare and technical trick combining the difficult laser flip with a precise slide entry.

name: Hardflip to Boardslide
history: A hardflip that lands in a boardslide. The opposing rotations of the hardflip set up the perpendicular board position for the slide.

name: Inward Heel to Noseslide
history: An inward heelflip into a noseslide. An exceptionally technical trick combining the inward heel with a precise nose lock.

name: Pivot
history: The front or back truck pivots 90 or 180 degrees on an obstacle without grinding. A transitional trick between regular riding and grinds.

name: Nose Pivot
history: The nose of the board pivots on the corner of an obstacle. The front truck serves as the pivot point.

name: Backside Pivot
history: A pivot performed backside (turning away from the obstacle). A clean and stylish trick on ledges and curbs.

name: Frontside Pivot
history: A pivot performed frontside (turning toward the obstacle). Creates a flowing line element in street skating.

name: Kickflip to 5-0
history: A kickflip into a 5-0 grind. The kickflip must be caught before locking the back truck into the grind position.

name: Heelflip to 5-0
history: A heelflip entry into a 5-0 grind. The heel flick sets up the grind by clearing the front truck over the obstacle.

name: Kickflip Smith
history: A kickflip into a Smith grind. A very technical trick that requires the board to flip and then lock into the precise Smith position.

name: Heelflip Smith
history: A heelflip into a Smith grind. The heel flick must be controlled precisely to land in the downward-angled Smith position.

name: Kickflip Feeble
history: A kickflip into a feeble grind. The board flips and lands with the back truck on the obstacle and front truck hanging over the far side.

name: Heelflip Feeble
history: A heelflip into a feeble grind. A technical rail trick combining the heel flip with the unique feeble lock.

name: Back Foot Impossible
history: An impossible where only the back foot wraps the board instead of the front foot. Creates a different visual and mechanical result from the standard impossible.
youtube_query: back foot impossible skateboard trick tutorial

name: One-Foot Kickflip
history: A kickflip executed with only the front foot on the board, the back foot hanging freely. A stylish variation requiring single-foot balance.
youtube_query: one foot kickflip skateboard trick tutorial

name: One-Foot Heelflip
history: A heelflip with only the front foot on the board. The back foot is kicked out as the board flips.

name: Banana Flip
history: A very obscure trick where the board curves through an arc during the flip motion, resembling the shape of a banana. Associated with creative freestylers.

name: Ghetto Grind
history: A grind on a non-traditional surface or obstacle, such as a rock, stump, or rough concrete edge. Popularized in the rough street spots of urban skating.

name: Hurricane 270
history: A hurricane grind followed by an additional 90-degree pivot to exit in a different direction. A creative and technical exit variation.

name: Nollie Heelflip 5-0
history: A nollie heelflip into a 5-0 grind. Combines the nollie heel with a precise back-truck grind entry.

name: Nosegrind 270 Out
history: A nosegrind that exits with a 270-degree body rotation. The extra 90 degrees beyond a half turn adds complexity.

name: Tailslide 270 Out
history: A tailslide that exits with a 270-degree spin. The additional rotation beyond 180 makes this a very technical slide exit.

name: Boardslide 270 Out
history: A boardslide that exits with a 270-degree spin. A creative and demanding exit from the perpendicular slide position.

name: Tailslide Kickflip Out
history: A tailslide that exits with a kickflip. Adding the flip trick to the slide exit dramatically increases the difficulty.

name: Noseslide Kickflip Out
history: A noseslide that exits with a kickflip. A technical slide-flip combination trick.

name: Lipslide Kickflip Out
history: A lipslide that exits with a kickflip. Combining the lipslide's body positioning with a flip exit.

name: 50-50 to Boardslide
history: A 50-50 grind that transitions into a boardslide mid-obstacle. The board pivots 90 degrees during the trick.

name: Nosegrind to Crooked Grind
history: A nosegrind that transitions into a crooked grind mid-obstacle. An extremely technical ledge trick.

name: Big Spin Nosegrind
history: A bigspin (360 body + 360 board) into a nosegrind. Combining the full spin combo with the precise grind makes this very technical.

name: Nollie Backside Heelflip
history: A nollie heelflip combined with a backside 180 body rotation from nollie stance.

name: Nollie Frontside Kickflip
history: A nollie kickflip combined with a frontside 180 body rotation. Also called a nollie frontside flip.

name: Fakie Frontside Kickflip
history: A kickflip with frontside 180 body rotation from fakie stance.

name: Fakie Backside Kickflip
history: A kickflip with backside 180 body rotation from fakie stance. A common advanced fakie combination.

name: Switch Frontside Kickflip
history: A frontside 180 kickflip in switch stance. The combination of the non-dominant stance with the spin-flip makes this very technical.

name: Switch Backside Kickflip
history: A backside 180 kickflip in switch stance. One of the higher-level switch tricks.

name: Switch Backside Heelflip
history: A backside 180 heelflip in switch stance. An advanced switch combination requiring mastery of both switch and heelflip mechanics.

name: Switch Frontside Heelflip
history: A frontside 180 heelflip in switch stance. Combines switch stance, frontside spin, and heelflip for maximum complexity.

name: Nollie Backside Kickflip
history: A backside 180 kickflip from nollie stance. A rare and technical nollie spin-flip combination.

name: Fakie Frontside Heelflip
history: A frontside 180 heelflip from fakie stance. Reverses all the standard mechanics of the regular frontside heelflip.

name: Fakie Backside Heelflip
history: A backside 180 heelflip from fakie stance. Combines reverse riding with the backside spin and heel flip.

name: Texas Plant
history: A footplant variation where one foot is extended out to the side while the other remains on the board. A stylistic old-school vert trick.

name: Fastplant
history: The front foot quickly plants on the coping while the skater goes up the ramp, then quickly re-mounts. A faster, snappier version of the boneless.
youtube_query: fastplant skateboard vert ramp trick tutorial
